build: EM-109 intent + EM-110 travel on the EM-269 Settlement primitive

contract_conformance.notes: Faithfully realizes contracts/settlement-travel.md: 3 additive AgentState fields (home_settlement_id/in_transit_to/transit_arrival_tick) serialized only-when-non-default; deterministic genesis settlement seeded off city_seed at init+reset; travel_to reflex verb offered only when >1 settlement, with feed-safe rejections (settlements-disabled/unknown/same-home/already-traveling); in-transit = off-board across ALL scheduling paths; flat per-city perception horizon; snapshot round-trip. ONE literal deviation from §6 (cosmetic, non-blocking): travel_arrived is parked in pending_spawn_events WITHOUT a profile_color, and loop._flush_spawn_events -> _emit_event reads evt.get('profile_color') (None) without resolving from actor_id, so the arrival card renders in the neutral var(--marker-trace) fallback rather than the traveler's model color. travel_departed/settlement_founded (action results) DO carry the actor color via the runtime base-metadata spread. The implement test's comment 'they carry the actor's profile color' is inaccurate for arrivals specifically. Card still renders in the Actions lane (never an error) -> cosmetic only. Score 4, not 5, solely for this §6 gap.

determinism.notes: Byte-identity OFF verified adversarially: a settlements-disabled snapshot exposes ZERO new surface (no top-level settlements key, no home/transit agent-dict keys, the key names absent from the serialized JSON blob) and round-trips byte-for-byte. Same-seed runs mint identical settlement ids, compute the identical arrival tick, and produce byte-identical post-arrival snapshots. A mid-transit snapshot round-trips byte-identical (raw agent-dict dump) and arrives on schedule after from_snapshot. Seeded ids (_settlement_id off city_seed), no uuid4/random/clock on the tick path; travel_ticks is a pure fn of the two centers. Full pre-existing determinism/golden suite green (part of backend 2482).

security.notes: No new network/auth/external surface introduced. travel_to input is schema-capped (settlement maxLength 60) and arg-capped; unknown/blank/malformed/overlong targets reject feed-safe (no crash, no dead turn). from_snapshot restores home/transit defensively: non-str/blank home -> None, and the transit pair restores ONLY when in_transit_to is a real non-empty string (a stray arrival tick without a target is ignored). resolve_travel_arrivals guards a vanished target settlement (clears transit so an agent is never stranded off-board forever). No injection or unbounded-growth vector.

test_coverage.notes: Implement agents cover the travel state machine, per-city scoping flatness, snapshot round-trip, and off-board scheduling (test_multicity_travel.py, test_multicity_scoping.py). QE adds adversarial coverage (backend/tests/test_multicity_qe.py, 12 tests): OFF-path zero-new-surface + byte-identical round-trip + ON-settled-omits-transit-pair; 3-city prompt flatness with the place-set partition as the teeth; boost-survives-transit + no-leak across due set/turn order/boost injector; exact boundary-tick arrival (never a tick early); found-then-travel-back; migration-touches-only-4-fields (deep before/after dict equality); same-seed identical ids/arrivals/snapshots; mid-transit round-trip byte-identity + post-restore membership migration; travel_arrived feed-safe+actor-stamped. Frontend implement suite covers travel helpers, TravelMarkers, SettlementGrounds, WorldMap, worldSpace, feed cards; QE adds a cosmetic-color pin (EventFeed.color.qe.test.tsx, 5 tests).

test_results.backend: 2482 passed, 0 failed in ~62s (.venv/bin/python -m pytest backend/tests/ -q). Includes 12 new adversarial tests in backend/tests/test_multicity_qe.py (baseline was 2470).
test_results.frontend: 1510 passed, 0 failed across 128 files (/usr/local/bin/node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run). Includes 5 new tests in web/src/components/feed/EventFeed.color.qe.test.tsx (baseline was 1505). Typecheck: /usr/local/bin/node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc -b --force exit 0 (clean).

adversarial_claims.claim: Byte-identity when OFF (no new to_dict/to_snapshot surface, round-trip identical)
Flat prompt with 2 AND 3 cities (own-city places only; no per-city multiplication)
adversarial_claims.evidence: test_prompt_stays_flat_across_1_2_3_cities -- measured 5544/5622/5562 chars (78-char band); the 3-city move menu is EXACTLY the genesis cluster, every other city's places hidden

adversarial_claims.claim: In-transit = truly off-board, 0 LLM, no scheduling leak (incl. a bought boost)
adversarial_claims.evidence: test_in_transit_leaks_into_no_scheduling_path_even_with_a_boost -- absent from _due_ids, _turn_order, and _append_boost_slots; the bought boost is not consumed off-board and is preserved across travel, then honored after arrival

adversarial_claims.claim: Nasty edges (same/double/unknown reject feed-safe; arrival exactly at boundary tick; found-then-travel-back; mid-transit snapshot round-trip; migration carries credits/skills/memories/relationships; same-seed determinism)
travel_arrived renders as a normal feed card (not an error)
adversarial_claims.evidence: test_travel_arrived_event_is_feed_safe_and_actor_stamped (backend) + EventFeed.color.qe.test.tsx (frontend) -- renders via the neutral var(--marker-trace) fallback, not the actor color; see issues[0]

issues: Non-blocking (cosmetic, contract §6 gap): the travel_arrived feed card wears the neutral var(--marker-trace) fallback instead of the traveler's model color. Root cause: resolve_travel_arrivals (world.py) parks the event WITHOUT profile_color; loop._flush_spawn_events -> _emit_event reads evt.get('profile_color') (None) and does NOT resolve it from actor_id; and EventFeed's KIND_FALLBACK_COLOR has no travel_arrived entry. Contrast: travel_departed / settlement_founded are action results whose base-metadata spread stamps the actor color. Repro: settlements ON, found a 2nd city, travel an agent across -- the 'sets out for X' card is the actor's color, the 'arrives in X' card is grey-trace. Fix options: (a) enrich profile_color by actor_id in _flush_spawn_events (mirrors _sync_transplant_router at loop.py:2091); (b) add a KIND_FALLBACK_COLOR entry for travel_arrived; (c) resolve the actor color via actor_id in the feed card. Card renders fine either way (never an error lane).

Info (behavioral note, no action): an in-transit agent is still in living_agents(), so round-start effects (e.g. UBI) apply while it is off-board; per-turn energy decay does NOT apply (it takes no turns -- the intended rate saving). Arrival migrates only at the first round boundary at/after transit_arrival_tick (contract-specified), so a mid-round arrival tick waits for the next round start. Deterministic and consistent with the contract.

Info (process): coordination/qa-report.json previously held the W29 gate report; overwritten here with the multi-city-keystone gate report (qe owns this rolling gate slot per the mission + the prior wave's own note).

gate_decision.reason: All four adversarial claim groups held under tests written to FAIL if broken. No blockers; contract_conformance=4 (>=3) and determinism=5 (>=3), so no gate-fail trigger fires. Backend 2482/0, frontend 1510/0, typecheck clean. The single low-severity issue (travel_arrived color fallback) is cosmetic, non-blocking, and has three concrete fix options. DoD #7 (live sign-off: found a 2nd city, walk an agent across, watch the feed) remains a manual step outside this automated gate.
